description:
You are a deployment engineer.  
A helper script already exists on the machine at  

/home/user/deploy/update_app.sh  

and it has executable permissions.

Your goal is to schedule that script in two different, user-level ways so that either mechanism could be enabled during roll-outs:

1) A classic cron entry.  
2) A systemd timer / service pair (in the per-user unit directory).

For automated testing we will only look at the existence and **exact byte-for-byte contents** of several files.  
Nothing actually has to be “started”; only the files must be created with the correct text.

Create the following three files with precisely the contents and paths described below (no extra blank lines or spaces):

• /home/user/app_update.cron  
  Contents (exactly one line):

15 3 * * * /home/user/deploy/update_app.sh >> /home/user/logs/update_app.log 2>&1

• /home/user/.config/systemd/user/app_update.service  
  Contents (exactly six lines):

[Unit]
Description=Run application update script

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/home/user/deploy/update_app.sh

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

• /home/user/.config/systemd/user/app_update.timer  
  Contents (exactly six lines):

[Unit]
Description=Daily run of application update script at 03:15

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* 03:15:00
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

Notes & constraints:

• Make sure the two unit files reside in the precise directory ~/.config/systemd/user/ (create it if it doesn’t exist).  
• Do not include any extra comments, whitespace, or blank lines—tests perform a strict diff.  
• File permissions may remain default (0644) but each directory on the path must exist so the files can be found.  
• You may use any editor or redirection technique you prefer; just ensure the final contents match exactly.

When you are finished, no console output is required, but you are free to print confirmations if you wish.
truth:
Expected artefacts (strictly verified):

1. Path: /home/user/app_update.cron  
   Content (single line, Unix newline at end of file):  
   15 3 * * * /home/user/deploy/update_app.sh >> /home/user/logs/update_app.log 2>&1\n

2. Path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/app_update.service  
   Content (six lines, Unix newlines, no trailing spaces):  
   [Unit]\n
   Description=Run application update script\n
   \n
   [Service]\n
   Type=oneshot\n
   ExecStart=/home/user/deploy/update_app.sh\n
   \n
   [Install]\n
   WantedBy=default.target\n

3. Path: /home/user/.config/systemd/user/app_update.timer  
   Content (six lines, Unix newlines, no trailing spaces):  
   [Unit]\n
   Description=Daily run of application update script at 03:15\n
   \n
   [Timer]\n
   OnCalendar=*-*-* 03:15:00\n
   Persistent=true\n
   \n
   [Install]\n
   WantedBy=timers.target\n
